title: Add polarity/tense grounding to digikoma-recap — wins are past-tense completed, not future-tense planned
description: Smoke test of digikoma-winddown against .tmp/recap-fixtures/sample-session.txt on 2026-05-14 surfaced a real correctness bug in digikoma-recap that the current word-overlap grounding gate cannot catch: the FM is writing BLOCKED items as if they were COMPLETED WINS. Fixture explicitly states 'apple-pi organism not yet authored', 'harness-schemas v0.2.0 bump not landed', 'gemini-session-schemas family doesn't exist on disk' — recap returned three wins titled 'Pi's Organism Authored', 'Harness-Schemas v0.2.0 Bump Landed', 'Gemini-Session-Schemas Family Authored'. Same items also surfaced (correctly) as caveats. Word-overlap grounding passes them because 'Pi', 'organism', 'authored' all appear in the transcript; what fails is the polarity/tense of the surrounding context. Fix candidates: (a) tighten @Guide on DigikomaRecapWin to explicitly say 'wins are work the transcript states was COMPLETED — never include blocked, planned, or pending work; that goes to openFollowups or caveats'; (b) add a Swift post-validator that for each win checks the transcript for negation patterns ('not yet', 'pending', 'blocked', 'TODO', 'needs to', 'will be') within a window around the matched anchor word, demoting matching wins to caveats; (c) ask FM for a structured tense field on each win that the validator can check. Recommended order: try (a) first as a smaller change, run the smoke test, if polarity flip persists then add (b). Also affects bugsCaught and decisions to a lesser degree — same polarity check applies.
resolutionNote: Both fix candidates landed. (a) @Guide tightening: DigikomaRecapWin/Bug/Decision @Generable descriptions now explicitly require past-tense / present-state observations. Wins must name COMPLETED work; bugs must name DISCOVERED issues; decisions must name RESOLVED forks. The descriptions name the failure mode explicitly so the FM has the rule in-context. (b) Polarity post-validator: new static `isPolarityFlipped(item:transcript:)` helper + 31-entry `polarityFlipMarkers` constant covering 'not yet', 'pending', 'blocked', 'doesn't exist', 'will be', 'needs to', 'still open/pending/blocked', 'todo', etc. The gate runs AFTER the anchor-overlap check inside groundRecap: for each anchor word the item shares with the transcript, scan an 80-char window around every occurrence; if any polarity-flip marker appears in the window, demote to caveats with `[ungrounded-polarity]` prefix. Applied to wins (load-bearing — the original failure mode), bugs, and decisions. Five new unit tests cover polarity detection in isolation + the integrated groundRecap path; all 11 unit tests pass. The fixture regression test (76s FM call) was not re-run in this commit but the gate doesn't change the test's tolerance bands; it only narrows what makes it past grounding. Substrate-floor caveat applies: word-context heuristic, not semantic — creative phrasing that doesn't include the marker substrings will still slip through.
